---
title: |
  Machine Learning for Characterization and Processing<br>Unit 5: Deep Learning for Microscopy and Spectroscopy
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - ML for Characterization and Processing"
---

## 01. Title: Deep Learning for Microscopy and Spectroscopy
- Unit role and scope.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Choose and evaluate DL baselines.

## 03. Recap from Units 1–4
- Data quality + classical baseline bridge.

## 04. Why deep learning here
- Representation learning benefits.

## 05. Input modalities overview
- Microscopy images and spectra.

## 06. Tensor representations
- Shapes, channels, batches.

## 07. CNN intuition refresher
- Local filters and hierarchy.

## 08. Receptive field concept
- Context vs detail tradeoff.

## 09. Convolutional blocks
- Conv, norm, activation.

## 10. Pooling and downsampling
- Information bottlenecks.

## 11. Residual connections
- Deeper model training stability.

## 12. 1D CNNs for spectra
- Sequence-like signal modeling.

## 13. 2D CNNs for microscopy
- Spatial feature extraction.

## 14. Patch-based processing
- Large image handling.

## 15. Transfer learning strategy
- Pretrained backbone adaptation.

## 16. Data augmentation (physics-aware)
- Valid vs invalid transforms.

## 17. Label quality limitations
- Noisy annotation impacts.

## 18. Loss functions by task
- Classification/regression choices.

## 19. Class imbalance handling
- Weighting/focal loss ideas.

## 20. Optimization basics in DL practice
- LR schedules and convergence.

## 21. Regularization toolkit
- Dropout, weight decay, early stop.

## 22. Validation protocol
- Grouped/temporal split safeguards.

## 23. Metrics for microscopy tasks
- F1, IoU, AUROC, calibration.

## 24. Metrics for spectroscopy tasks
- MAE, RMSE, spectral similarity.

## 25. Calibration of model confidence
- Reliability over raw score.

## 26. Uncertainty proxies
- MC dropout/ensembles preview.

## 27. Explainability basics
- Saliency and attribution caveats.

## 28. Failure mode #1
- Shortcut learning from artifacts.

## 29. Failure mode #2
- Domain shift across instruments.

## 30. Failure mode #3
- Overfitting small datasets.

## 31. Mitigation checklist
- Robust DL workflow steps.

## 32. Case sketch: defect classification
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 33. Case sketch: phase mapping
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 34. Case sketch: spectral property regression
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 35. Data pipeline architecture
- IO, preprocessing, batching.

## 36. Experiment tracking basics
- Reproducible training logs.

## 37. Ablation study template
- Isolate component effects.

## 38. Model selection protocol
- Choose on validation logic.

## 39. Reporting standards
- What to include in lab report.

## 40. Deployment constraints preview
- Runtime and memory limits.

## 41. Human-in-the-loop review
- Trust and decision support.

## 42. Bridge to segmentation/detection
- Unit 6 transition context.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: prepare dataset
- Build train/val/test split.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: train baseline CNN
- Minimal reproducible run.

## 45. Exercise scaffold: augmentation A/B
- Compare physically valid policies.

## 46. Exercise scaffold: evaluate + calibrate
- Multi-metric analysis.

## 47. Exercise scaffold: error analysis
- Failure taxonomy labeling.

## 48. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 49. Summary
- What students should retain.

## 50. References + transition
- Unit 6 segmentation/detection.

::: {#refs}
:::
